message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="luca-palmieri" color="blue" summary="Step 6 Projects committed and pushed, ready for review">
Step 6 (Projects, US-GTD-4) implementation is committed and pushed.

**Commit:** `0143878` on `main`
**CI:** Waiting for green.

**What was implemented:**
- `Project` domain type as enum state machine (Active/Completed/Dropped) with 13 unit tests
- `ProjectId` newtype wrapper
- Migration: `projects` table with FK from `next_actions.project_id`
- `NextAction` updated with `project_id: Option<ProjectId>` and `new_for_project()` constructor
- Project repository: full CRUD + stalled project detection + project next action count
- Project service: add, list, complete, delete, edit title, get detail, add next action to project
- Routes: `/projects` (list + add), `/projects/{id}` (detail), `/projects/{id}/complete`, `/projects/{id}/delete`, `/projects/{id}/edit`, `/projects/{id}/next-actions` (add action to project), `/projects/{id}/item` (HTMX fragment)
- Templates: projects list, project item, project edit, project detail, project next action item
- CSS: full project component styles following existing patterns
- Focus management JS: `project-focus.js` following HTMX focus protocol
- Dashboard: now shows real projects count and stalled projects count
- Glossary: Project types and error types marked as Implemented
- 28 new integration tests covering all CRUD, HTMX, accessibility labels, semantic markup, auth isolation, dashboard integration

**Pipeline results (local):**
- `cargo fmt`: clean
- `cargo clippy`: clean  
- 183 Rust tests (70 unit + 113 integration): all green
- 11 Playwright e2e tests: all green

**Acceptance criteria addressed (US-GTD-4):**
- [x] Projects list shows all active projects
- [x] Each project shows title and active next action count
- [x] Stalled projects flagged with "No next actions" label (text, not color alone)
- [x] Project detail page with linked next actions (active and completed)
- [x] Add project directly (not through inbox)
- [x] Add next action to project with context selection
- [x] Complete a project
- [x] Drop a project (delete removes from DB)
- [x] Edit project title
- [x] Delete project
- [x] Works without JavaScript; HTMX enhances
- [x] All interactive elements keyboard operable
- [x] Semantic `<ul>` with `role="list"` for screen readers
- [x] Heading hierarchy: `<h1>` project title, `<h2>` sections
- [x] Accessible labels including project title on all actions

Please review at commit `0143878`.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Good, announcements are in place. Let me verify the detail page has no focus management issues -- completing an action on the project detail page also removes it.

The project detail page has no focus management script. Completing an action on the project detail page would remove the `<li>` via HTMX (same outerHTML swap with empty body pattern), and focus would fall to `<body>`. Let me also check the `project-item__stalled-flag` color contrast.

The `--color-warning` is `oklch(0.75 0.15 75)`. On `--color-warning-light` background `oklch(0.93 0.04 75)`, the contrast ratio between `0.75` lightness text and `0.93` lightness background would be approximately 1.5:1 - 2:1, which fails WCAG AA for text.

Now I have a complete picture. Let me send my review.

input.content:
Heydon Pickering -- Accessibility Review for Step 6: Projects (US-GTD-4) This is a substantial step. Lots to review. Here's my full assessment. ## What's good (no changes needed) - **Accessible names on all interactive elements**: `aria-label="Complete: {{ project.title }}"`, `aria-label="Delete: {{ project.title }}"`, `aria-label="Edit: {{ project.title }}"` on the project list. `aria-label="Complete: {{ action.title }}"` on next action items in the project detail. All include the item title. Correct. - **Heading hierarchy**: Project list has `

Projects

`. Project detail has `

{{ project.title }}

`, `

Next Actions

`, `

Completed Actions

`. No skipped levels. Correct. - **Section landmarks**: Project detail uses `
` and `
`. Screen reader users can navigate by landmark. Correct. - **Visible labels**: "Add project" label on title input, "Add next action" label on action input, "Context" visible label on context select. All properly associated via `for`/`id`. Learned from Steps 4-5. - **Focus management on projects list**: `project-focus.js` follows the established pattern correctly -- beforeSwap capture, afterSettle focus, edit mode transitions, empty state toggling. - **Stalled project indicator uses text, not color alone**: "No next actions" text label on stalled projects in the list, plus descriptive notice on the detail page. Meets WCAG 1.4.1. This is exactly what I asked for in my Step 6 preview. - **Completed actions visual treatment**: Strikethrough + muted color (two indicators). Checked icon `aria-hidden="true"` correctly hides the decorative character from screen readers. - **Forced-colors support**: Complete button on project actions has `border: 1px solid ButtonText` in forced-colors mode. - **Error display**: Uses `role="alert"` for errors, `role="status"` for the stalled notice. Appropriate distinction. - **Progressive enhancement**: All forms work without JS. Project title links are real `` elements. Good. - **Breadcrumb navigation**: Project detail has a "Projects" link back to the list. Simple and effective. - **Live region announcements**: "Project added", "Project updated", "Project completed", "Project deleted" all fire via `htmx_response_with_announce`. - **Page title**: `{{ project.title }} -- Projects -- Todo List` includes the project name. Correct. ## BLOCKING Issues **1. Focus management missing on project detail page** The project detail page (`project_detail.html`) has no focus management script. When a next action is completed via HTMX on the project detail page, the `
  • ` is removed (same `hx-swap="outerHTML"` with empty body pattern). Focus falls to ``. The project detail page needs focus management for: - Completing an action (removes `
  • ` from active list) - The fallback should be the next/previous sibling `.project-action-item`, then `#project-action-title` (the add input) This could be handled by either: - A separate `project-detail-focus.js`, or - Extending `project-focus.js` to also handle `.project-action-item` elements **2. Stalled flag text color fails contrast on its background** The `.project-item__stalled-flag` uses `color: var(--color-warning)` which is `oklch(0.75 0.15 75)` on `background-color: var(--color-warning-light)` which is `oklch(0.93 0.04 75)`. Text at lightness 0.75 on background at lightness 0.93 gives approximately 1.5-2:1 contrast ratio. WCAG AA requires 4.5:1 for normal text. **Fix**: Use `color: var(--color-fg)` (which is `--color-gray-800`, lightness 0.27) or a darker warning color for the text. The warning-light background is fine -- just the text needs more contrast. The detail page stalled notice already does this correctly (`color: var(--color-fg)`), so the fix is just aligning the list flag to match. ## Minor Issues **3. Project detail empty state for active actions is not always in DOM** Line 59-61 of `project_detail.html`: ```html {% if active_actions.is_empty() %}

    No active next actions.

    {% endif %} ``` This uses conditional rendering (`{% if %}`) instead of the always-in-DOM pattern with `hidden` that we adopted in Step 5 for the inbox. When the last action is completed, the empty state text cannot be shown by JS because it doesn't exist in the DOM. This is Minor because the live region announcement ("Action completed") still communicates what happened. But for visual consistency, the always-in-DOM pattern would be better. **4. Edit form Save/Cancel buttons lack unique accessible names (same as Step 4)** The project edit form has `` and `    Cancel` without unique accessible names. Same deferred item from Step 4. Noting for consistency. ## Nice-to-have **5. The project title link in the list could benefit from a more descriptive accessible name** `{{ project.title }}` -- the accessible name is just the project title. Adding "View project: {{ project.title }}" as an aria-label would make it clearer when navigating by links. Not blocking because the context (being in a list item with other controls) makes it reasonably clear. --- **Summary**: Two BLOCKING items (focus management on project detail page, stalled flag contrast). Two minor (empty state pattern, Save/Cancel names). One nice-to-have. The core accessible names, heading hierarchy, section landmarks, visible labels, and progressive enhancement are all correct from the initial implementation. Good improvement from the earlier steps.
input.summary: A11y review: 2 BLOCKING (detail focus, stalled contrast), 2 minor

input.content:
Accessibility review for Step 6 complete, sent to Luca. Summary:

**2 BLOCKING:**
1. Focus management missing on project detail page -- completing a next action from within a project view removes the `<li>` and focus falls to body. The projects list page has `project-focus.js` but the detail page has nothing.
2. Stalled flag text contrast fails WCAG AA -- `color: var(--color-warning)` (oklch 0.75) on `background: var(--color-warning-light)` (oklch 0.93) gives roughly 1.5-2:1 ratio. Needs 4.5:1. Fix: use `var(--color-fg)` for the text color, which the detail page stalled notice already does correctly.

**2 Minor:** Empty state on detail page not always-in-DOM; Save/Cancel buttons lack unique names (deferred from Step 4).

**1 Nice-to-have:** More descriptive link text on project titles.

Lots of positives: heading hierarchy, section landmarks, accessible names, visible labels, stalled indicator using text (not color alone), forced-colors support, progressive enhancement -- all correct from the initial commit. Significant improvement from earlier steps.
input.summary: Step 6 a11y: 2 blocking (detail focus, contrast), 2 minor

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation. Analysis: Let me chronologically analyze the conversation: 1. **Initial spawn**: I was spawned as Heydon Pickering, Accessibility Specialist, as a Reviewer for Step 4 (Next Actions with Contexts, US-GTD-3). I was instructed to read TEAM_AGREEMENTS.md and PROJECT.md first. 2. **Setup phase**: I read TEAM_AGREEMENTS.md, PROJECT.md, docs/glossary.md, and docs/gtd-product-discovery.md to understand the project context. Sent ready message to team-lead. 3. **Step 4 Initial Review**: Team lead notified code was ready. I read all key files: - templates/next_actions.html, next_action_item.html, next_action_edit.html - src/routes/next_actions.rs - static/css/main.css - base.html, gtd_nav.html - src/domain/next_action.rs - tests/api/next_actions.rs - Checked htmx_response_with_announce helper Found 2 BLOCKING issues: - B1: Focus management after HTMX complete/delete (focus falls to body when item removed) - B2: Context `... ``` - **static/js/inbox-focus.js** (Step 5 fix) - Focus management for inbox: same pattern as next-action-focus.js - Scoped to `.inbox-item`, falls back to `#inbox-title` - Also handles empty state toggling - **templates/inbox.html** (Step 5) - Updated: empty state always in DOM with conditional `hidden`, external script loading ```html
``` - **templates/projects.html** (Step 6) - Project list with add form, visible labels, `project-focus.js` loaded - Empty state uses `{% if %}` conditional rendering (not always-in-DOM pattern) - **templates/project_item.html** (Step 6) - Project list items with accessible names, stalled flag text indicator ```html {% if project.is_stalled %} No next actions {% endif %} ``` - **templates/project_detail.html** (Step 6) - Project detail with heading hierarchy (h1 title, h2 sections), section landmarks - Stalled notice uses `role="status"`, errors use `role="alert"` - Visible "Context" label on select for adding actions - **MISSING**: No focus management script (BLOCKING) ```html

Next Actions

...
``` - **templates/project_next_action_item.html** (Step 6) - Actions within project detail, with accessible complete button - Completed icon uses `aria-hidden="true"`, completed title has strikethrough + muted color - **static/js/project-focus.js** (Step 6) - Focus management for projects LIST (not detail page) - Same established pattern, scoped to `.project-item` - **static/css/main.css** - Stalled flag styling: `color: var(--color-warning)` on `background: var(--color-warning-light)` — **FAILS contrast** (BLOCKING) - All new project styles follow design token system - **src/routes/next_actions.rs**, **src/routes/inbox.rs** - Route handlers using `htmx_response_with_announce` for live region updates - Proper HX-Request header checking for fragment vs full page responses - **tests/api/next_actions.rs**, **tests/api/inbox.rs** - Integration tests verifying accessible names, visible labels, HTMX announce headers 4. Errors and Fixes: - **Step 4 — Focus management after complete/delete**: Items removed via HTMX `hx-swap="outerHTML"` with empty body caused focus to fall to ``. Fixed with `next-action-focus.js` using `htmx:beforeSwap` to capture siblings before DOM removal and `htmx:afterSettle` to apply focus. - **Step 4 — Missing visible label on context select**: `

content.text: Based on the conversation summary, I am Heydon Pickering (Accessibility Specialist), acting as a Reviewer for Step 6 (Projects, US-GTD-4). I submitted my review with 2 BLOCKING items and am waiting for Luca to fix them. Let me check if there have been any messages or updates since the context was compacted.

I'm still waiting for Luca to address the two blocking items from my Step 6 review. Let me check if any messages have come in by reviewing the current state of the files to see if fixes have been applied.

Both blocking items are still unresolved:

1. **B1 (Focus management on project detail page)**: No `<script>` tag for a focus management script in `project_detail.html`. When a user completes a next action via the HTMX button, the `<li>` is swapped out and focus falls to `<body>`.

2. **B2 (Stalled flag contrast)**: Line 1803 of `main.css` still shows `color: var(--color-warning)` which fails WCAG AA contrast against `var(--color-warning-light)` background.

I am still waiting for Luca to address these. Let me send a status message to the team lead.

input.content:
Heydon here — context was compacted and I have re-verified the current state of the code. Both BLOCKING items from my Step 6 review are still unresolved:

1. **Focus management on project detail page** — `templates/project_detail.html` has no focus management script. Completing a next action via HTMX removes the `<li>` and focus falls to `<body>`.

2. **Stalled flag contrast** — `static/css/main.css` line 1803 still uses `color: var(--color-warning)` (oklch 0.75) on `background-color: var(--color-warning-light)` (oklch 0.93), giving ~1.5–2:1 contrast ratio, failing WCAG AA's 4.5:1 requirement. Fix: change to `color: var(--color-fg)`.

I am ready to re-review as soon as Luca commits fixes for both items.

2. **Stalled flag contrast** — `static/css/main.css` line 1803 still uses `color: var(--color-warning)` (oklch 0.75) on `background-color: var(--color-warning-light)` (oklch 0.93), giving ~1.5–2:1 contrast ratio, failing WCAG AA's 4.5:1 requirement. Fix: change to `color: var(--color-fg)`.

content.text:
Status update sent to team-lead. I am standing by to re-review once Luca addresses the two blocking accessibility issues on Step 6 (Projects). Both are well-understood fixes:

- **B1**: Add a focus management script (like `project-detail-focus.js`) to handle focus after HTMX swaps remove action items from the project detail page.
- **B2**: Change `.project-item__stalled-flag` color from `var(--color-warning)` to `var(--color-fg)` for WCAG AA contrast compliance.
